title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
statement_text: Let $t \geq 1$ be an integer, and let $\mathcal{G}$ be a minor-closed class of graphs. Then $\chi(\mathcal{G}) \leq t$ if and only if $K_{t+1} \notin \mathcal{G}$.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture is presented as the motivating characterization of minor-closed classes by chromatic number. The paper pursues a clustered analogue: bounding the clustered chromatic number of minor-closed classes, with the sequel proving the clustered weakening $\chi^\star(\mathcal{X}(K_t)) = t-1$.
paper_contribution: This paper provides the technical foundations (island theory, clustered coloring numbers) that the companion sequel uses to prove the clustered weakening $\chi^\star(\mathcal{X}(K_t)) = \chi^\star_{\ell}(\mathcal{X}(K_t)) = t-1$; the conjecture itself is not proved here.
notes: PDF source — '6∈' in the extraction is ∉; statement reads cleanly.
paper_title: Islands in minor-closed classes. I. Bounded treewidth and separators

title: Esperet–Ochem Conjecture 3
context_text: Kleinberg–Motwani–Raghavan–Venkatasubramanian and, independently, Alon–Ding–Oporowski–Vertigan established $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}(\Sigma)) \geq 4$ for every surface $\Sigma$. Esperet and Ochem conjectured that this lower bound is tight, i.e., the clustered chromatic number of the class of graphs embeddable on any fixed surface equals exactly four.
paper_contribution: Fully proved as Corollary 13: for every surface $\Sigma$, $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}(\Sigma)) = \chi^\star_{\ell}(\mathcal{G}(\Sigma)) = \mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G}(\Sigma)) = 4$, via Corollary 12 applied to the Euler-formula density bound $|E(G)| \leq 3|V(G)| + c(\Sigma)$.
notes: Referenced as [11, Conjecture 3] but not restated verbatim; statement reconstructed from Corollary 13 and surrounding prose.
paper_title: Islands in minor-closed classes. I. Bounded treewidth and separators

title: Esperet–Ochem Conjecture 7
context_text: The question of determining $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}_5(\mathbb{R}^2))$ for planar graphs of girth at least five was open until this work. Esperet and Ochem conjectured the value 2 for the clustered coloring number of surface-embeddable graphs of girth at least five.
paper_contribution: Fully proved as Corollary 14: for every surface $\Sigma$, $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}_5(\Sigma)) = \chi^\star_{\ell}(\mathcal{G}_5(\Sigma)) = \mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G}_5(\Sigma)) = 2$, using the density bound $|E(G)| \leq \tfrac{5}{3}|V(G)| + c(\Sigma)$ from Euler's formula.
notes: Referenced as [11, Conjecture 7] but not restated verbatim; statement reconstructed from Corollary 14 and surrounding prose.
paper_title: Islands in minor-closed classes. I. Bounded treewidth and separators

title: Conjecture 4
statement_text: A minor-closed class of graphs $\mathcal{G}$ satisfies $\mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G}) \leq t$ if and only if there exists $m \geq 1$ such that $K_{t,m} \notin \mathcal{G}$ and $I_{t-1}+P_m \notin \mathcal{G}$.
context_text: Observation 3 shows that any class $\mathcal{G}$ with $\mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G}) \leq t$ can contain only finitely many graphs of the form $K_{t,m}$ or $I_{t-1}+P_m$; the conjecture asserts that this necessary condition is also sufficient for minor-closed classes. Theorem 7 confirms the conjecture for the special case of minor-closed classes of bounded treewidth, and Conjecture 4 would imply $\chi^\star_{\ell}(\mathcal{G}) = \mathrm{col}^\star(\mathcal{G})$ for every minor-closed class.
notes: PDF source — '6∈' in the extraction is ∉; statement reads cleanly.
paper_title: Islands in minor-closed classes. I. Bounded treewidth and separators
